_note: Aesop's Fables 改編，公有領域。每篇控制在 60-120 字，A1-A2 難度。vocab 用 ECDICT 等級的中翻，內含本篇可能對謙恩有點挑戰的字。

stories.title: The Lion and the Mouse
stories.text:
A small mouse ran across a sleeping lion. The lion woke up and grabbed the mouse. "Please let me go!" cried the mouse. "I will help you one day." The lion laughed but let the mouse go.

A week later, the lion was caught in a hunter's net. He roared loudly. The little mouse heard him. She came quickly and chewed the ropes. The lion was free!

"Thank you, little friend," said the lion. "Small friends can be great friends."

comprehension.q: Why did the lion let the mouse go at first?
choices: The mouse promised to help him one day
The mouse was too small to eat
The mouse ran away too fast

comprehension.q: What was the lion caught in?
What does this story teach us?
choices: Small friends can help in big ways

stories.title: The Tortoise and the Hare
stories.text:
A hare laughed at a slow tortoise. "You are so slow! I am much faster than you," he said. The tortoise said, "Let's have a race."

The race began. The hare ran very fast and got far ahead. "That tortoise is so slow. I have time to take a nap," he thought. He lay down under a tree and fell asleep.

The tortoise kept walking, step by step. He never stopped. When the hare woke up, the tortoise was already at the finish line.

Slow and steady wins the race.

comprehension.q: Why did the hare take a nap?
choices: He thought the tortoise was too slow

comprehension.q: What is the lesson of this story?
choices: Slow and steady wins the race

stories.title: The Fox and the Grapes
stories.text:
One hot day, a hungry fox saw some grapes hanging high on a tree. They looked sweet and juicy.

"I want those grapes!" said the fox. He jumped up high, but he could not reach them. He tried again and again. He jumped and jumped, but the grapes were too high.

Finally, the fox gave up. He walked away and said, "Those grapes are probably sour anyway. I didn't really want them."

When we cannot get what we want, we often pretend we never wanted it.

comprehension.q: Why did the fox want the grapes?
choices: He wanted to take them home

comprehension.q: Why couldn't the fox get the grapes?
choices: The grapes were too high to reach

comprehension.q: What did the fox say when he walked away?

stories.title: The Boy Who Cried Wolf
stories.text:
A young boy watched sheep on a hill. He was bored, so he shouted, "Wolf! Wolf! A wolf is eating the sheep!"

The villagers ran up the hill to help. But there was no wolf. The boy laughed at them.

A few days later, he did it again. "Wolf! Wolf!" The villagers came running, but again, no wolf. They were angry.

Then one day, a real wolf came. The boy shouted, "Wolf! Wolf! Help me!" But this time, nobody believed him. Nobody came. The wolf ate all the sheep.

If you lie too often, no one will believe you when you tell the truth.

comprehension.q: Why did the boy shout "Wolf!" the first time?
choices: He was bored and wanted some fun
He was scared of the dark

comprehension.q: What happened when a real wolf came?
choices: Nobody believed him, and the sheep were eaten
The boy fought the wolf alone
The wolf ran away by itself

comprehension.q: What is the lesson of this story?
choices: If you lie too often, no one will believe the truth

stories.title: The Ant and the Grasshopper
stories.text:
In summer, a grasshopper sang and danced all day. An ant walked by, carrying food on his back.

"Why do you work so hard?" asked the grasshopper. "Come sing with me!"

"I am storing food for winter," said the ant. "You should do the same."

The grasshopper laughed. "Winter is far away. There is plenty of time."

Soon, winter came. The grasshopper had no food. He was cold and hungry. He went to the ant's house.

"Please, can you give me some food?" he asked.

The ant said, "You sang all summer. Now you can dance all winter."

comprehension.q: What was the grasshopper doing in summer?
Why didn't the grasshopper store food for winter?
choices: He had no food to store
He thought winter was far away

comprehension.q: What did the ant say to the grasshopper in winter?
choices: "You sang all summer, now dance all winter"
